name: Unit
what_it_does: Embedded banking-as-a-service API enabling fintechs to offer FDIC-insured accounts, debit and credit cards, ACH, wires, and checks within their own product.
use_cases: Embedding FDIC-insured bank accounts inside a fintech or SaaS product
Issuing branded debit or charge cards for corporate spend management
Offering ACH and wire transfers under your own brand via Unit's infrastructure
Building a neobank product without becoming a bank
not_for: Companies that just need payment initiation without embedded account features
Simple B2B wire or ACH-only use cases without account issuance
best_when: You are building a fintech application that needs to offer your end users or businesses a full banking experience (accounts + cards + transfers) under your brand.
avoid_when: You only need payment initiation APIs without account issuance, or you need direct Federal Reserve access without a banking partner intermediary — Increase is a better fit.

auth.notes: Org-level API keys for server-to-server calls. Customer tokens (JWT) for end-user-scoped operations with limited permissions. Scopes control access to specific resource types.

pricing.paid_tiers: Per-application partnership pricing; custom contracts. Sandbox environment available during evaluation. Revenue share or per-transaction model negotiated based on volume.
pricing.notes: Unit requires a partnership agreement before production access. Evaluation sandbox is available after an introductory call. Not self-serve for production.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply but are not publicly documented. Discussed during partnership onboarding. Sandbox limits are lower than production.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses follow a consistent JSON:API-aligned format with error type codes and detail fields. Quality is good but not as comprehensive as Increase's error taxonomy.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Idempotency keys supported on all payment creation endpoints via X-Idempotency-Key header. Duplicate detection window is 24 hours.
known_agent_gotchas: Customer token (JWT) scoping adds complexity for agents — org-level vs customer-level permissions must be carefully managed to avoid authorization errors.
Card issuance flows have compliance steps (KYC/KYB) that are async and must be polled for completion before card operations are available.
Rate limits are undisclosed publicly — agents operating at high throughput should request documented limits during partnership onboarding.
Partnership requirement means no self-serve path to production; coordinate production go-live with Unit's team in advance.

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC 2 Type II and PCI DSS compliant. JWT-based customer tokens enable least-privilege access for end-user operations. TLS enforced. Scoped tokens reduce blast radius for compromised credentials.
